mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and elevated recession risk, causing investors to price in lower corporate revenues and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, which amplifies aggregate demand growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or housing starts decouple from equity returns during financial stress.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of observations during mid-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations rise above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across all tested rolling windows, or the signal is fully subsumed by FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 230.0, or inflation spikes fail to predict equity drawdowns after adjusting for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic expansion and robust household purchasing power, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal produces a negative or flat forward return distribution, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95.0, manufacturing contraction signals supply-side weakness and impending earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.
